zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh de Pêche於大約1220年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可格亞特 比阿林斯時，他的父親Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne29歲，母親Eve de Peverell27歲。他於1242年與Ida de Hastings在英國米德萊可倫敦結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1291年1月16日在英國英格蘭素福爾可利斯布利其享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pêche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a variant of   Pech   ‘pitch, tar’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Peče or of its Germanized form Petsche , which is also a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname.

variants.name: Pech
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Alvaro, Diego, Edelmira, Emilio, Francisco, Gilberto, Jorge, Jose Angel.  German Gerhard, Hans, Ilse, Lutz, Manfred, Wolf.

Czech and Slovak; Sorbian (Pěch and P'ech): from a pet form of the personal name Petr (Czech), Pětš (Lower Sorbian), and Peter (Slovak). This is also a Germanized form of the Upper Sorbian cognate Pjech.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German pech (German Pech) ‘pitch, tar’, presumably a nickname for someone with exceptionally dark skin or hair, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who prepared, sold, or used pitch. This may in part also be the origin of a homonymic Polish, Czech, and Slovak surname (compare above and below).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Pêche was born about 1220, in Great Bealings, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne, was 29 and his mother, Eve de Peverell, was 27. He married Ida de Hastings in 1242, in London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died about 16 January 1291, in Risbridge, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh de Pêche est né environ 1220, à Great Bealings, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Eve de Peverell, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Ida de Hastings en 1242, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 16 janvier 1291, à Risbridge, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.Great Bealings, Woodbridge, Suffolk, Eng: Great Bealings, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh de Pêche um 1220 in Great Bealings, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne 29 und seine Mutter Eve de Peverell 27. Er hat 1242 in London, Middlesex, England Ida de Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb um 16. Januar 1291 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Risbridge, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Pêche è nato il circa 1220 nel Great Bealings, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Eve de Peverell, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Ida de Hastings nel 1242, nel London, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 16 gennaio 1291, nel Risbridge, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Pêcheが約1220年にイギリスイギリススフォルクグレアト ビーリングズで生まれた時，父Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourneは29歳，母Eve de Peverellは27歳であった。1242年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンでIda de Hastingsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1291年1月16日にイギリスイギリススフォルクリスブリッジで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Pêche(이)가 대략 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 그리트 빌링스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve de Peverell(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1242년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 Ida de Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1291년 1월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 리스브리지에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh de Pêche родился около 1220 г. в местности грит билингс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hamon De Pecche II Baron Of Bourne было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Eve de Peverell, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Ida de Hastings в 1242 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер около 16 января 1291 г. в местности рисбридж, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет.
